calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Finalizing The Trust
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for petitioner Richard Stephen Broussard, who was also present (via Zoom).
Darin Leviloff for temporary co-trustees Rebecca Guyette and Maria Mandolini (via Zoom).
Kelly Perreault for Rebecca Guyette and Maria Mandolini as co-conservators of person and estate (via Zoom).
Franchesca Callejo, court-appointed attorney for settlor/conservatee Dorothy Lewis (via Zoom).
Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard, former co-trustees (via Zoom).
Rebecca Guyette, co-trustee/co-conservator, was present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status of Winding Up Invalidated Trust.

Ms. Perreault reported that review of the trust drafting file and subpoenaed financial records had been completed, and that additional financial accounts potentially held in the name of the trust or the settlor individually had been identified. She reported that the co-conservators were in the process of contacting financial institutions to determine whether the accounts remained active and, if so, to marshal any remaining assets into the conservatorship estate. She further reported that efforts were ongoing to retitle real property into the conservatorship estate, including property located in California and Louisiana, with the assistance of local counsel.

Mr. Leviloff concurred with Ms. Perreault's report.

Mr. Adams inquired whether the Court would retain jurisdiction to address any remaining issues related to winding up the invalidated trust and requested clarification as to whether a supplement could be filed, rather than a standalone petition, if further court action were required.

Following discussion, the Court authorized the filing of a supplement, rather than a new petition, to address the issues described in paragraph nine of the Court's October 29, 2025 order. The Court further ordered that the status hearing regarding winding up of the invalidated trust be taken off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Off Calendar

Petition To 1) Compel Trustees To Disclose Trust Assets And Terms 2) Remedy Breach Of Trust By Trustees; 3) Compel An Accounting; 4) Suspend And Remove Trustees; 5) Appoint Petitioner As Sole Trustee; 6) Order Payment Of Attorneys' Fees; 7) Reduce Trustee Compensation; And 8) Prohibit Use Of Trust Funds To Defend
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Austin Adams for petitioner Richard Stephen Broussard.
Jason Williams, respondent in Pro Per.
Darin Leviloff for and with temporary co-trustees Maria Mandolini, and Rebecca Guyette.
G. Franchesca Callejo for settlor Dorothy Lewis.

At the outset, the court notes the issue regarding the lack of personal service of the order to appear and produce documents and related orders to show cause. There is additional discussion regarding Richard Stephen Broussard's Petition to compel, etc. Counsel and Mr. Williams presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the instant petition. At the conclusion of discussion, the court advises that it will be taking the matter under submission with the intent to prepare its own order after hearing as follows:

1) Deem all objections/responses waived by both respondents per CRC 7.801, after confirming that no responsive pleading was filed by the 10/22 deadline set by the Court at the 9/22 hearing (and specified in the Court's 9/25 order served on both respondents).

2) Invalidate the settlor's power of attorney that designated respondent Patrice Broussard as attorney-in-fact with authority to create a trust for the settlor.

3) Invalidate the subject irrevocable trust created by respondent Patrice Broussard as attorney-in-fact under the invalidated power of attorney.

4) Direct the temporary co-trustees to wind up the affairs of the invalidated trust and transfer all assets from the invalidated trust to the temporary or general conservators of the estate.

5) Order respondent Patrice Broussard to account as attorney-in-fact under the invalidated power of attorney.

6) Deny the requests, as unnecessary, to remove respondents and upgrade the status of the temporary co-trustees to general successor co-trustees, in light of the invalidated trust only requiring its affairs to be wound up by the temporary co-trustees.

7) Deny the request to reform the trust as unnecessary, that the trust is invalidated, but the settlor's conservators are free to petition for substituted judgment in the related PCN case to create a new trust.

8) Deny the request to invalidate the settlor's pour-over will, the settlor's conservators are free to petition for substituted judgment in the related PCN case to revoke the will (and create a new will).

9) Deny without prejudice the request to appoint a guardian ad litem for the settlor, in light of pending appointment of the settlor's general conservators in the related PCN case.

10) Reserve the Court's determination of all other issues involving damages against, or recovery of any specific property from, either respondent.

11) Allow petitioner or trustees to return with a new petition with proof of damages and tracing of property after they complete their independent investigation and discovery.

Mr. Leviloff asks the court to confirm Mr. Williams' address, Mr. Williams confirms that his and Patrice Broussard's address is P.O. Box 2086, San Francisco, CA 94126.

The Petition to Compel, etc., is taken under submission. The remaining subject matters are taken off calendar.

A Hearing on the status of winding-up the invalidated Trust is set for Jan-05-2026 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Robles. Not Reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear And Produce Documents
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Off Calendar

Petition To 1) Compel Trustees To Disclose Trust Assets And Terms 2) Remedy Breach Of Trust By Trustees; 3) Compel An Accounting; 4) Suspend And Remove Trustees; 5) Appoint Petitioner As Sole Trustee; 6) Order Payment Of Attorneys' Fees; 7) Reduce Trustee Compensation; And 8) Prohibit Use Of Trust Funds To Defend
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Continued To Oct-27-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for petitioner Richard Stephen Broussard, (via Zoom).
Jason Williams, respondent in Pro Per (via Zoom).
Darin Leviloff for temporary co-trustee Maria Mandolini, who was also present in court.
G. Franchesca Callejo for settlor Dorothy Lewis. (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the status of trust administration and compliance with prior court orders. Counsel reported that former co-trustees Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard had not cooperated and had still not produced any documents to date.
Mr. Williams argued that the petitioner lacked standing and asserted, that he had failed to provide proof of his relationship to the settlor, that he has not turned over any documents, that he has been out of town and is still out of town, and that he is in touch with the property manager and has instructed him to provide rent rolls and insurance information to the fiduciary.

Ms. Mandolin requested the white binder to be turned over to her, it contains all the medical history of Ms. Lewis.

Mr. Adams raised concerns regarding the durable power of attorney, and it is still being used by Patrice Broussard on Ms. Lewis' behalf while the conservatorship is in place. He is requesting the durable power of attorney be suspended. The Court granted this request.

Ms. Polo's office to submit an order suspending the durable power of attorney.

The Court finds Jason Williams was aware of the court order to produce documents and failed to obey the court order. The Court set a hearing to determine willfulness.
An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Patrice Broussard (co-respondent/co-trustee/POA agent for settlor) to appear on October 27, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions should not be imposed against her for failure to appear and for failure to comply with past orders. First Amended Petition etc., and Order to Appear and Produce Documents are also continued to that date.

Judge: Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear And Produce Documents
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Continued To Oct-27-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Trust Administration
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Compliance With Feb-25-2025 Order
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Off Calendar

Petition To 1) Compel Trustees To Disclose Trust Assets And Terms 2) Remedy Breach Of Trust By Trustees; 3) Compel An Accounting; 4) Suspend And Remove Trustees; 5) Appoint Petitioner As Sole Trustee; 6) Order Payment Of Attorneys' Fees; 7) Reduce Trustee Compensation; And 8) Prohibit Use Of Trust Funds To Defend
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Continued To Sep-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for petitioner Richard Stephen Broussard, who was also present (via Zoom).
Jason Williams, respondent in Pro Per (via Zoom).
Darin Leviloff and Mia Polo for temporary co-trustees Rebecca Guyette and Maria Mandolini, who were also present (via Zoom).
G. Franchesca Callejo for settlor Dorothy Lewis.

Discussion was held regarding the status of trust administration and compliance with prior court orders. Counsel reported that former co-trustees Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard had not cooperated and had still not produced any documents to date. Mr. Adams also reported that subpoenaed financial records were outstanding and that the trust properties had been left in derelict condition.

Mr. Williams argued that the petitioner lacked standing and asserted that he had failed to provide proof of his relationship to the settlor. Mr. Adams responded that the standing issue had been argued repeatedly and that written objections due September 15, 2025 were not filed. Mr. Adams requested an order deeming Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard to have waived objections under CRC 7.801.

Ms. Guyette requested an order for an accounting. The Court granted the request.

Ms. Polo raised concerns regarding the durable power of attorney and whether it was still being used by Patrice Broussard on Ms. Lewis' behalf while the conservatorship is in place.

The Court addressed the related conservatorship matter and extended existing orders until further order.

Following discussion, the Court issued the following orders:

1) Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard are ordered to appear in person on September 24, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, with all documents previously ordered produced. All counsel are ordered to appear to review the produced documents on site for completeness. If production is incomplete, Mr. Williams and Ms. Broussard shall leave the courthouse, retrieve the missing documents, and return the same day to complete production. This process shall continue until production is completed. Mr. Adams subpoenaed documents are not subject to this order.

2) Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard shall provide an accounting no later than October 22, 2025.

3) Notwithstanding prior deadlines, the Court extends the deadline for Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard to file any verified objections or responses from September 15, 2025 to October 22, 2025. Failure to timely respond will result in objections being waived pursuant to CRC 7.801.

4) The October 2, 2025 hearing on Rebecca Guyette and Maria Mandolini's Petition for Appointment of Conservator of the Person and Estate (in case PCN-25-308603) shall be continued to October 30, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to coincide with the related petition. All existing orders regarding temporary conservatorship are extended until further order of the Court.

5) Hearings on status re: compliance with February 25, 2025 order and status re: trust administration are off calendar.

The Court stated that any outstanding issues not discussed at this time would be addressed at the September 24, 2025 hearing.

Judge: Lydia Villarreal; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDERS AND ORDER SETTING THRESHOLD HEARING ON STANDING.
Off Calendar Sep-03-2025 Denied

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Trust Administration
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Continued To Sep-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Compliance With Feb-25-2025 Order
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Continued To Sep-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Darin Leviloff for Temporary Co-Trustees Rebecca Guyette and Maria Mandolini, who were also present (via Zoom).
Austin Adams for Petitioner Richard Broussard, who was also present (via Zoom).
Jason Williams, Respondent (via Zoom).

Mr. Leviloff reported that the trust had been mismanaged by former trustees, that assets in California and Louisiana were being marshalled, and that a temporary conservatorship had been secured for settlor Dorothy Jean Bernstein Stubblefield-Lewis to ensure her safety.

Mr. Adams reported that the former co-trustees had failed to file or serve objections to his amended petition by the August 4 deadline and stated his intention to seek default.

Mr. Williams stated that he and Patrice Broussard intend to renew objections to Mr. Broussard's standing, and argued that competent evidence of his descent from the settlor had not been established and requested a threshold hearing on that issue. The Court advised that no formal objection was on file and directed Mr. Williams to file any objections by September 15, 2025, noting that under California Rules of Court, rule 7.801, objections not timely filed may be deemed waived.

Mr. Williams further requested a stay of proceedings pending resolution of standing. The Court denied the oral request for stay.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to September 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Richard Wayne Broussard, son-in-law to Dorothy Lewis and father of Patrice and Richard Broussard (via Zoom).

The Court called the matter for continued status and to address the recently filed amended petition by Mr. Adams. Mr. Leviloff reported developments regarding the settlor, Dorothy Lewis, including a failed welfare visit, subsequent police welfare check, and brief hospitalization. Mr. Leviloff informed the Court of his intent to seek conservatorship of both person and estate.

The Court and parties discussed the procedural background, including noncompliance with prior orders and the financial and health-related risks facing the trust and Ms. Lewis. Mr. Williams spoke at length, disputing the basis for his and Patrice Broussard's suspension as trustees and asserting that they were not afforded sufficient opportunity to respond. He indicated an intent to secure counsel but acknowledged ongoing difficulty in doing so due to financial constraints.

Mr. Leviloff requested the Court consider referring the matter to the Public Guardian under Probate Code Sec. 2920. The Court declined to issue such an order at this time but encouraged Mr. Leviloff to contact the Public Guardian with a status report and proposed conservatorship plan. The Court stated it would evaluate any forthcoming conservatorship petition upon filing.

Mr. Richard Wayne Broussard addressed the Court and expressed his willingness to care for Dorothy Lewis personally. The Court thanked him and confirmed that any future caretaking decisions would depend on developments in the case. Mr. Broussard obtained Mr. Leviloff's contact information during the hearing so that they may open a dialogue.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to August 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Adams was informed that his amended petition would be addressed at the future hearing, and was directed to serve the amended petition on Jason Williams and Patrice Broussard. Any opposition or responsive documents from Jason Williams and/or Patrice Broussard were ordered due by August 4, 2025.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Compliance With Feb-25-2025 Order
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Continued To Jun-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for Richard Broussard, Petitioner.
Darin Leviloff for and with Rebecca Guyette and Maria Mandolini, Temporary Trustee and Proposed Co-Temporary Trustee.
Jason Williams, Co-Respondent in Pro Per.

The Court inquired about the status of the pending petition challenging the validity of the trust. Mr. Adams indicated that he intends to file the amended petition by the end of next week. The Court addressed Mr. Williams, who has not complied with the order to turn over trust documents or property, as previously ordered, resulting in a continued daily sanction of $250. Mr. Williams expressed difficulties in complying with the order, citing financial hardship and lack of legal representation. He claimed that his and Patrice Broussard's lives were disrupted by the suspension order.

Ms. Guyette reported significant difficulties in administering the trust due to the lack of cooperation from the suspended trustees and the severe financial distress of the trust assets. The Court authorized the appointment of Maria Mandolini as a co-temporary trustee with full authority, alongside Ms. Guyette, to assist with trust administration.

The Court ordered Mr. Williams to schedule a meeting between Dorothy Lewis and Ms. Guyette and/or Ms. Mandolini within one week and to ensure the meeting occurs by the end of May. The Court emphasized that Ms. Lewis is entitled to receive financial support from the trust for her care, and encouraged cooperation to ensure her needs are met.

After discussion, the court continued all three matters to June 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and indicated that an Order After Hearing will be issued as to the appointment of Ms. Mandolini as Co-Temporary Trustee and to address sanctions against suspended trustees.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar May-07-2025 Continued To Jun-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To 1) Compel Trustees To Disclose Trust Assets And Terms 2) Remedy Breach Of Trust By Trustees; 3) Compel An Accounting; 4) Suspend And Remove Trustees; 5) Appoint Petitioner As Sole Trustee; 6) Order Payment Of Attorneys' Fees; 7) Reduce Trustee Compensation; And 8) Prohibit Use Of Trust Funds To Defend
Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Continued To May-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for Richard Broussard, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Nathan Pastor for Patrice Broussard and Jason Williams (via Zoom).
Darin Leviloff (in-person) for and with Rebecca Guyette (via Zoom), Temporary Trustee.
Patrice Broussard for herself as Suspended Co-Trustee and as DPOA for Dorothy Lewis.
Jason Williams, Suspended Co-Trustee (via Zoom).

Patrice Broussard and Jason Williams raised no objections to Mr. Pastor's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel. There being no objections, the court granted Mr. Pastor's Motions to be Relieved as Counsel.

The Court discussed the pending petition to compel an accounting, remove trustees, appoint a sole trustee, and related relief. Mr. Adams stated he intends to file an amended petition within four weeks, potentially challenging the validity of the trust.

The Court addressed compliance with its February 25, 2025 order requiring the former trustees to surrender all trust property and records to Ms. Guyette. Mr. Leviloff reported that no documents had been produced. Mr. Williams attributed noncompliance to difficulties securing counsel but expressed intent to comply.

The Court ordered that all trust property and records be turned over to Ms. Guyette by close of business on April 9, 2025, and that a $250/day sanction for each day of noncompliance shall be imposed on Patrice Broussard and Jason Williams thereafter should they fail to meet the deadline.

The court continued the matter to May 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for Patrice Broussard and Jason Williams to secure new counsel and for Mr. Adams to file an amended petition. The court set status hearings regarding compliance with the February 25, 2025 order and regarding trust administration by the temporary trustee.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To 1) Compel Trustees To Disclose Trust Assets And Terms 2) Remedy Breach Of Trust By Trustees; 3) Compel An Accounting; 4) Suspend And Remove Trustees; 5) Appoint Petitioner As Sole Trustee; 6) Order Payment Of Attorneys' Fees; 7) Reduce Trustee Compensation; And 8) Prohibit Use Of Trust Funds To Defend
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Continued To Mar-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for Richard Broussard, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Nathan Pastor for Patrice Broussard and Jason Williams, Respondents (via Zoom).
Jason Williams, co-trustee, appeared in court.
Stephen Broussard appeared via Zoom.

A discussion was held regarding Mr. Paster's recently submitted Motion to be Relieved as Counsel. Mr. Paster stated that, due to a conflict of interest, he was unable to participate substantively and had no objection to Mr. Williams addressing the Court directly. Mr. Williams stated that the co-trustees were seeking new counsel and had a meeting scheduled for later in the day. He further reported that they had secured a loan commitment to refinance a defaulted mortgage, which they expected to close within 17 business days. He also stated that the lender holding the defaulted loan had granted a 30-day forbearance, which they were in the process of finalizing.

Mr. Adams disputed Mr. Williams' claims, stating that at the January 6, 2025 hearing, co-trustees' counsel assured the Court that outstanding financial issues had been resolved through refinancing. However, he asserted that he repeatedly inquired about the foreclosure status and received no response. He indicated that the trust still has outstanding debts, including a foreclosure sale scheduled for 2/28/2025. He also alleged that co-trustee Patrice improperly signed loan documents under protest, leading to the lender denying the refinance loan. Additionally, he raised concerns about trust mismanagement, citing $1.4 million in unexplained withdrawals from trust-owned properties, the lender's doubts about how those funds were used, and over $65,000 in additional trust debts.

The Court ruled that, under Probate Code Sections 15642(e) and 17206, it would suspend co-trustees Patrice and Jason Williams, effective immediately, and appoint Rebecca Guyette as temporary trustee, effective upon posting of bond. Bond was set at $6 million, with instructions that the bond may be increased if needed based on further marshaling of trust assets. Mr. Adams was ordered to prepare an order reflecting this ruling and send it to the Probate Examiner.

The court continued the matter to March 26, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Nathan Pastor for Patrice Michelle Broussard and Jason Sherrod Williams, Co-Trustees and moving parties.
Austin Adams for Richard Stephen Broussard, Petitioner and opposing party.
Stephen Broussard is present on Zoom.
Jason Williams is present in the courtroom.

Mr. Pastor reported that there has been a breakdown in the attorney-client relationship and a motion to withdraw as counsel was recently submitted for filing with the court. Therefore, he requested a continuance of the hearing. Mr. Adams opposed the request for continuance, arguing that the co-trustees had engaged in delay tactics and requested the court to rule on the motion to dismiss immediately.

The court denied the motion to dismiss, adopting the tentative ruling. An order will be prepared by the court.

Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Continued To Feb-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for Richard Broussard, Petitioner/Beneficiary (via Zoom).
Nathan Pastor for Patrice Broussard and Jason Williams, Respondents/Co-Trustees (via Zoom).
Jason Williams, Co-Trustee, present in court.

Mr. Pastor reported progress in trust administration, including resolving issues with property blight, mortgage refinancing, and utility payments. Mr. Adams argued the issues arose during the co-trustees' management and were not inherited. The court noted that Mr. Pastor's prior assurances of resolution lacked documentation and urged Mr. Pastor to provide more evidence of proper trust administration.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Retention Of Counsel By Respondents
Off Calendar Dec-16-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Austin Adams for Richard Broussard, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Nathan Pastor for Patrice Broussard and Jason Williams, Respondents (via Zoom).

Mr. Pastor confirmed his recent retention (Friday) as counsel for respondents and indicated he is reviewing the case and intends to file an appearance, an objection, and potentially a motion to dismiss. The court takes the status hearing regarding retention of counsel by respondents off calendar.

Mr. Adams raised concerns regarding trust mismanagement, including foreclosure notices, unpaid taxes, and other financial issues affecting trust assets. He also reported receiving threatening messages from Patrice Broussard, directed at his client and the court.

Mr. Pastor acknowledged the issues and proposed that he and Mr. Adams can meet and confer to address concerns. He suggested that his clients should continue to manage the trust.

The court expressed concern over the current state of trust assets, emphasizing the need for swift action to prevent further mismanagement. The court continued the petition to January 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and ordered that a copy of the trust must be provided to Mr. Adams by December 18, 2024. Parties shall meet and confer to resolve short-term issues. A written update on the status of the case and efforts to address concerns must be submitted prior to the next hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
